items.title: Restoration Campaign Across Duhok Highlights Kurdistan's Growing Archaeological Ambitions
items.desc: International partnerships, local expertise, and long-term conservation projects are transforming Duhok into a leading center for archaeological research, heritage preservation, and sustainable cultural tourism.

items.title: 20 Kurdish Farmers Face Trial Over Land Dispute in Khurmatu
items.desc: Lawsuits filed by relocated Arab settlers bring decades-old land ownership disputes in the Kurdistani Territories outside KRG Administrative control back before the courts.

items.title: Oil Climbs Above $90 as U.S.-Iran Conflict Deepens, Raising Global Supply Concerns
items.desc: Renewed U.S.-Iran military escalation and mounting concerns over the Strait of Hormuz are driving oil prices higher while raising fresh questions about inflation, shipping security, and global economic stability.

items.title: Industry revival key to diversifying Iraq’s economy, experts say
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq’s efforts to revive its industrial sector will determine whether the country can reduce its dependence on oil revenues and build a more productive economy, industry officials and economic analysts interviewed by Shafaq News suggested, calling for greater focus on restarting factories, supporting local production and attracting investment. items.title: Drone crashes in al-Faw district south of Basra
items.desc: Shafaq News- Basra A drone crashed on Monday in the al-Faw district of Basra province, in southern Iraq, causing no casualties or material damage, a security source told Shafaq News. The aircraft came down near Ras al-Bisha, inside an abandoned farm, after a technical malfunction. It did not detonate on impact, and security units removed it from the site. items.title: Diyala seizes 127K smuggled medicines
items.desc: Shafaq News- Diyala Health inspectors in Iraq's eastern Diyala province blocked an attempt to bring 127,000 smuggled medicines into the province, the Health Directorate said on Monday. The shipment was seized from a vehicle after inspectors found it in breach of storage and transport requirements and without official testing or regulatory clearance. items.title: Tehran hails PM Al-Zaidi’s visit to advance ties
items.desc: Shafaq News- Tehran Iran on Monday welcomed Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi’s upcoming visit to Tehran as an important diplomatic step in relations with Baghdad. Speaking during his weekly press conference, Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei described the visit as an opportunity for engagement between the two neighboring countries, reaffirming Tehran’s commitment to Iraq&rs

items.title: Asayish arrests two ISIS militants in Al-Sulaymaniyah
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil The Kurdistan Region’s Security Agency (Asayish) apprehended two ISIS militants in two separate operations in Al-Sulaymaniyah province and the Raparin Administration. items.desc: The latest update from the US Food and Drug Administration means the exact source of the parasite is still not known.

items.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.desc: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.

items.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
items.desc: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Keir Starmer resigns, Andy Burnham to become U.K. prime minister
items.desc: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham is replacing Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
items.desc: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

items.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
items.desc: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.
